playlists.title: Project Walkthrough
playlists.tagline: Defend your decisions — deployment, tests, and PRs included
playlists.description: The onsite question that replaces 'reverse a binary tree': walk through a real project end-to-end. Six episodes on the 10-minute spine, platform choice, test strategy, PR evidence, failures, and Reflection — aligned with ChamiNexT Ship Tests.

episodes.title: The 10-minute spine
episodes.problemYouSolve: Stop feature-dumping — tell a decision story with a hook, stakes, and a clear ending.
sampleQuestions: Walk me through the most impactful project you've worked on recently.
Pick one project — what was the problem, what did you build, and what was the outcome?
You have 10 minutes — how do you structure your answer?
episodes.strongAnswerFramework: Hook (user + metric + constraint) → context + non-goals → 2–3 decisions (not features) → execution snapshot → one failure → results vs baseline → Reflection. Use 'I decided' not 'we built.'
strongSignals: Names non-goals (what you deliberately did not build)
Ends with a specific Reflection, not generic 'more testing'
weakPatterns: No baseline metric — impact is unmeasurable
episodes.coachTip: Interviewers interrupt early if you're boring. Front-load the hook — they decide in 90 seconds if they'll dig deeper.

episodes.title: Key decisions & trade-offs
episodes.problemYouSolve: Prove you chose under constraints — and can name what you rejected.
sampleQuestions: What were the main architectural decisions, and what alternatives did you consider?
Why did you choose this approach over [obvious alternative]?
If you had half the time, what would you cut from scope?
episodes.strongAnswerFramework: DTS per decision: Option A vs B → criteria (latency, ops, team, deadline) → choice → downside accepted → mitigation. Musk lens: what did you delete before optimizing?
strongSignals: Names rejected alternative with honest trade-off
Ties decision to constraint (team size, deadline, scale)
weakPatterns: Optimizing a component that shouldn't exist
episodes.coachTip: Staff interviewers listen for delete-before-optimize. Say what you cut from v1.

episodes.title: Deployment & platform choice
episodes.problemYouSolve: Answer 'how did this run in prod?' — not just 'how did it compile locally.'
sampleQuestions: Where did you deploy this, and why that platform?
How did you roll out changes safely?
What was your rollback plan when something went wrong?
episodes.strongAnswerFramework: Platform + why (latency, cost, compliance, time-to-ship) → rollout (canary, flags, CI/CD) → observability (golden signals, dashboards) → rollback trigger → why NOT [K8s/serverless/monolith] at your scale.
strongSignals: Explains platform fit to constraints, not resume keywords
Names rollout phases and rollback criteria
Observability before or with launch — not after incident
weakPatterns: Picked trendy stack without scale justification
episodes.coachTip: ChamiNexT ships Netlify + serverless functions — a valid exemplar for learners. Ship Tests accept optional preview deploy URLs.

episodes.title: Unit tests & quality gates
episodes.problemYouSolve: Show tests protect real failure modes — not checkbox coverage.
sampleQuestions: How did you test this before production?
What would break if you removed your unit tests?
Walk me through a bug your tests caught.
episodes.strongAnswerFramework: Test pyramid: unit (pure logic) → integration (contracts) → load/chaos/evals (ML) → what each layer caught. Name one test tied to a real incident or near-miss.
strongSignals: Specific test classes, not 'we had coverage'
Load or chaos before canary when scale matters
ML: eval harness with regression threshold
weakPatterns: Tests added after production incident with no lesson
episodes.coachTip: Ship Test tickets require pytest/unit tests — practice the same language in the interview.

episodes.title: PRs as evidence
episodes.problemYouSolve: Use pull requests to show how you think — not how busy you were.
sampleQuestions: Walk me through a PR you're proud of.
How did you break a large change into reviewable pieces?
What review feedback changed your approach?
episodes.strongAnswerFramework: PR arc: RFC/design → vertical slice → hardening → rollout. Per anchor PR: problem, key diff philosophy, review comment that improved it, test evidence in CI. Never lead with PR count.
strongSignals: Describes review dialogue, not solo genius
PR description documents trade-offs and AI/tool disclosure when relevant
weakPatterns: Cannot explain one PR in detail
episodes.coachTip: VERVE-style hiring uses Work Tickets — your Ship Test PR *is* interview practice for this round.

episodes.title: Failures & Reflection
episodes.problemYouSolve: Pass the seniority filter — learn in public, not perform perfection.
sampleQuestions: Tell me about something that went wrong after launch.
What would you do differently if you started this project today?
What did you learn that changed how you work on the next project?
episodes.strongAnswerFramework: STAR+R on failure: detect → diagnose → fix → metric impact → Reflection (specific process/tech change, not 'communicate more'). One honest v2 improvement.
strongSignals: Quantifies impact of failure and fix
Reflection changes future behavior (observability week 1, idempotency day 1, etc.)
episodes.coachTip: Reflection is the +R in STAR+R (career-ops). One sentence can raise the hire bar.
